description_long: This object consists of a central, tiered stone structure resembling a small monument or altar, surrounded by several elongated and rounded stone blocks of varying sizes. The main structure is built from weathered, gray stone blocks, stacked in a stepped, pyramidal fashion with two rectangular openings on the front face. The surrounding blocks are arranged loosely around the base, some appearing cylindrical and others more rectangular, with a rough, aged surface texture. The stone surfaces show signs of moss or lichen growth, particularly along the edges and crevices, giving the object an ancient, timeworn appearance. The overall construction suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban or park setting, possibly as a decorative or protective feature. The arrangement and weathering patterns evoke a sense of historical or archaeological significance.
description: A weathered, tiered stone monument surrounded by rough, moss-streaked stone blocks, evoking an ancient or archaeological barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main stepped stone structure with two rectangular openings and surrounding stone blocks arranged loosely.
description_view_1: Direct frontal view highlighting the symmetrical arrangement of the stone blocks and clear visibility of the monument's openings.
description_view_2: Side-angled view revealing the depth of the main structure and the placement of the surrounding blocks, especially on the left.
description_view_3: Rear-angled view focusing on the back and right side of the monument, with the surrounding blocks forming a partial enclosure.
attribute_location_context: ancient temple foundation or archaeological park feature
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked and positioned on compacted earth or stone base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible moss and lichen growth along edges and crevices, with some surface erosion and discoloration indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: loosely grouped with adjacent stone blocks forming a partial enclosure
